title: Offensive AT Field Use
wikitext:
[]: Offensive AT. Field Use             Quote Post by SHINOBI-03 » Mon Mar 21, 2011 2:32 pm So, we all remember the moment in episode 19 when Unit 01 awakens, Zeruel attacks, and #01 counters by slashing both Zeruel's arm and it's A.T. Field by waving her hand. Now, for a while, I was puzzled with this attack and wondered how it happened. But then, I remembered when Asuka used her A.T. Field as an attack in the End Of Evangelion movie. So, my question is: Was Unit 01's attack at that time the same as Asuka's attack in the movie? Did she used her A.T. Field to counter Zeruel's attack and cut through it's field?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Mar 21, 2011 3:30 pm That's the implication, yeah.

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Mon Mar 21, 2011 3:44 pm Yup. It's been explicitly stated that an AT Field can be used offensively and defensively, so there's no reason why they couldn't be the same attack (or similar attacks, at the very least).

[]:             Quote Post by xdiesp » Wed Mar 23, 2011 9:21 pm To me, that scene with Zeruel looks like his AT got up so desperately fast it cut its hand. Word of God is none on the matter. :p

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 24, 2011 3:08 am xdiesp wrote: To me, that scene with Zeruel looks like his AT got up so desperately fast it cut its hand.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Thu Mar 24, 2011 5:22 pm That's what I was thinking as an offensive AT Field sounds like a really useful thing to have so if they'd have the ability to use one without help from mama I certain they would have.

[]:             Quote Post by User-iel » Sat Mar 26, 2011 3:20 am Of course I will accept this attack as yet another AT field 'technique', just one of many over the course of the series. Do you agree that there seem to be slashes for the extended digits? And no orange light this time, either. I wish to assert that the portrayal has been directly inspired by an old favorite from Japanese story telling, and a substantial number of anime sword-fighting scenes. "Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi", the famous sword that directs wind, and ancestor of the "wind cutter" idea. I lack any reference to prior works involving Anno containing a similar reference, but perhaps this detail was contributed by other Gainax staff at the time. I have not checked for a corresponding proposal or script, and if the visual action is described. Your mileage may vary.

[]:             Quote Post by User-iel » Tue Mar 29, 2011 7:23 am ^^

[]:             Quote Post by Sik » Tue Mar 29, 2011 5:09 pm User-iel wrote: A comment on this thread is now located here: [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=441921&highlight=#441921[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by LoCooLaidman » Sat Apr 02, 2011 6:35 pm If you can deploy an A.T Field to disrupt another A.T Field, then shouldn't you be able to split a person using the same method? I imagine that an A.T field is kind of like a weapon that can cut through the soul, mind, and body.
